Container.Name: itsweber-send
Container.Overview:
End-to-end encrypted file sharing, self-hosted in a single Docker container. Files are encrypted in the browser (AES-256-GCM, key in URL fragment) before they touch the server — the server only ever sees ciphertext. Resumable chunked uploads handle files of arbitrary size; downloads stream straight to disk via the File System Access API where supported. Optional accounts unlock 2FA (TOTP with scannable QR), API tokens, audit log, per-user quotas and an admin panel.
THREE DEPLOYMENT MODES
1) Behind your existing reverse proxy (NPM, Traefik, external Caddy, Nginx). Default for this template. Set REVERSE_PROXY_MODE=true (default), point your proxy at host:3000 over plain HTTP. Set ORIGIN/BASE_URL to your public URL.
2) LAN direct (no public domain). Set REVERSE_PROXY_MODE=false and use the bundled HTTPS port 8443 with the embedded Caddy + self-signed cert.
3) Public with bundled Caddy + Let's Encrypt. Use docker-compose.yml from the repo instead of this template.
FIRST-TIME SETUP (read this before clicking Apply)
The container runs as a non-root user (UID 10001). The host directory you pick for /data must be owned by that UID. Open the Unraid terminal and run:
mkdir -p /mnt/user/appdata/itsweber-send chown -R 10001:10001 /mnt/user/appdata/itsweber-send
Without this, SQLite cannot open the database file and the container exits on start with a clear chown hint in the log.
When using REVERSE_PROXY_MODE=true behind NPM, set Network to a custom static-IP bridge (e.g. br1) and clear the Port mapping below — your reverse proxy reaches the container directly on its assigned LAN IP.
LICENSE: AGPL-3.0-only. DOCS: https://github.com/ITSWEBER-OFFICIAL/itsweber-send#readme
Container.Changes:
### v1.3.5 (2026-05-05) - Polished Unraid template for Community Apps submission: TemplateURL, Date, Changes block, tighter Overview.
### v1.3.4 (2026-05-05) - Release pipeline temporarily builds linux/amd64 only. arm64 returns once GitHub provides native arm64 runners on the free tier.
### v1.3.3 (2026-05-05) - Hamburger drawer for the account / admin sub-navigation on phone-sized viewports. - README + Unraid template overview now document the post-apply cleanup step (`rm /boot/config/plugins/dockerMan/templates-user/itsweber-send.xml`).
### v1.3.2 (2026-05-05) - Fixed 2FA TOTP code input: Svelte template parser had been mangling `pattern="[0-9]{6}"` into `pattern="[0-9]6"`, blocking both 2FA enable and 2FA login. - Mobile responsive fixes on /account, /admin and /account/quota stat grids.
### v1.3.1 (2026-05-05) - QR code in the 2FA setup flow — scan straight from the screen into Aegis / Google Authenticator / Bitwarden / 1Password. - Unraid Docker template (`unraid/itsweber-send.xml`) for one-shot install. - Startup preflight check in the entrypoint: prints an actionable chown hint when /data is not writable instead of crashing with the cryptic SQLITE_CANTOPEN. - Mobile responsive fixes for the appbar wordmark on narrow phones.
### v1.3.0 (2026-05-05) - `REVERSE_PROXY_MODE=true` environment variable: skips the embedded Caddy and binds Node directly on `0.0.0.0:3000` so the container can sit behind an existing reverse proxy without double TLS termination. - New `docker/docker-compose.proxy.yml` and `docs/REVERSE_PROXY.md` with copy-paste snippets for NPM, Traefik, external Caddy and vanilla Nginx. - Repaired `docker/docker-compose.yml` so the bundled external Caddy can actually reach the send container. - `/health` endpoint now reports the version from `apps/api/package.json` instead of a stale hardcoded string.
### v1.2.0 (2026-05-04) - All-in-one Docker image with embedded Caddy. - SMTP mailer for first-download notifications, configurable through the admin panel. - FSA streaming for single-file downloads with per-file progress. - Cross-session upload resume. - Visible build version in the app footer.

Config.Name: Reverse Proxy Mode
Config.Description: true: skip embedded Caddy, bind Node on 0.0.0.0:3000 for an external reverse proxy. false: enable embedded Caddy on 8443 with a self-signed cert (LAN-only).

Config.Name: Public URL
Config.Description: The public URL the service is reachable under. Used to build share links and as the cookie scope. Match what your reverse proxy serves.

Config.Name: Base URL
Config.Description: Base URL for the SvelteKit frontend. Set the same value as Public URL.

Config.Name: Log Level
Config.Description: Pino log verbosity: trace, debug, info, warn, error, fatal.

Config.Name: Rate Limit Per Minute
Config.Description: Per-IP request limit per minute. Healthcheck endpoints are exempt.

Config.Name: Enable Accounts
Config.Description: Allow optional user accounts on top of anonymous uploads.

Config.Name: LAN Hostname (mode 2 only)
Config.Description: Only used when REVERSE_PROXY_MODE=false: hostname or LAN IP that gets baked into the embedded Caddy's self-signed certificate. Browse via https://<value>:8443.

Config.Name: Data
Config.Description: SQLite database (shares.db) plus encrypted uploaded blobs. Pre-chown the host directory to 10001:10001 before the first start (see Overview).

Config.Name: HTTP Port (reverse-proxy mode)
Config.Description: Plain HTTP port. Map this when using REVERSE_PROXY_MODE=true and Bridge networking; clear it when using a custom static-IP network like br1.

Config.Name: HTTPS Port (mode 2 only)
Config.Description: Self-signed HTTPS via embedded Caddy. Only map when REVERSE_PROXY_MODE=false.
